result: If I had the power and freedom to change the world—just one change—I would tune our global mindset toward true empathy. Not the vague idea of “be nice,” but a deeply felt, everyday instinct to see the humanity in each other before anything else. I believe shifting our default attitude toward genuine compassion would ripple out into how we build systems, solve conflicts, care for the planet, and treat ourselves. With empathy rooted at the core, progress in every other area would become more natural, more sustainable, and more just.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
